name: Logiwa WMS Cloud Fulfillment API
what_it_does: Logiwa REST API for cloud-native warehouse management system (WMS) and fulfillment platform targeting e-commerce and 3PL operations. Enables AI agents to manage inventory receipt and putaway workflow automation, handle pick, pack, and ship workflow orchestration for order fulfillment, access real-time inventory level and location data for stock management, retrieve order fulfillment status and shipping label generation, manage return processing and restocking workflow automation, handle multi-channel order routing and fulfillment priority management, access carrier rate shopping and shipping carrier selection, retrieve warehouse productivity and fulfillment analytics, manage multi-warehouse and multi-client (3PL) operations data, and integrate WMS data with Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, and e-commerce order management platforms.
use_cases: Routing orders to optimal warehouse from fulfillment automation agents
Tracking inventory levels from replenishment agents
Processing returns from reverse logistics agents
Integrating WMS with e-commerce platforms from order management agents
not_for: Manufacturing WMS without e-commerce fulfillment and direct-to-consumer focus
Heavy industrial or pharmaceutical WMS without DTC e-commerce context
On-premises WMS without cloud-native multi-client architecture requirements
best_when: A DTC brand or 3PL using Logiwa wants AI agents to automate order fulfillment workflows, inventory management, return processing, carrier selection, and e-commerce platform integration.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: WMS automation errors cause mis-picks, incorrect shipments, and inventory discrepancies — all automated receiving and shipping workflows require validation against expected data. Inventory adjustments without physical count reconciliation create permanent discrepancies. Carrier selection automation requires validated rate negotiation data.

auth.notes: Logiwa uses OAuth 2.0 and API key authentication. Per-account credentials. Developer portal at developer.logiwa.com. Webhooks for fulfillment event notifications. REST API with JSON responses. Multi-client architecture for 3PL operations. Native integrations with 50+ e-commerce channels. ShipStation and EasyPost carrier integrations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — monthly subscription based on order volume
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-order and monthly minimums; 3PL client billing included in enterprise plans
pricing.notes: Chicago, Illinois. Founded 2017. Private ($25M funding). Cloud WMS market for e-commerce and 3PL. 350+ customers. Strong in DTC brand fulfillment and 3PL operators. Multi-channel e-commerce integration focus. Competes with ShipBob and Deposco for cloud-native WMS market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: WMS automation errors cause shipment failures and inventory discrepancies; validate all automated order data before submission; errors are difficult to reverse after picking begins
Multi-client 3PL architecture — if operating as 3PL, all operations require client scoping; validate client ID mapping before bulk operations
Webhook for fulfillment events — use webhooks for shipment, receiving, and return completion events; polling order status for large fulfillment volumes is inefficient
Carrier integration dependencies — carrier rate shopping and label generation depends on carrier credential configuration; verify carrier credentials before automation
Inventory adjustment audit — all automated inventory adjustments require reason code; maintain audit trail for inventory accuracy reconciliation
Returns processing — automated return receiving must validate return against original order; automated restocking without quality check can add defective inventory
agent_readiness.security_notes: Cloud WMS. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA. API key/OAuth2. US. Inventory, order, and fulfillment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: WMS fulfillment. SOC2, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. US. Warehouse inventory and fulfillment data.
